title: Maintainability Scan
findings: Found 2 maintainability issues: retired install implementation still coexists with the live install-v2 entry, and generated client hook copies are checked into the package alongside templates.

issues_found.title: Retired install implementation remains beside live install-v2 and is still tested
issues_found.description: The command registry routes production `fabric install` to `./install-v2.js`, but the older `packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts` still exports a full `installCommand` and `runInitCommand` implementation with the same command metadata. Tests still import the retired module directly, so behavior can diverge between tested legacy code and the shipped command path, making maintenance and regression triage ambiguous.
issues_found.fix_direction: Either delete/quarantine `commands/install.ts` and migrate the remaining tests/helpers to `install-v2` or shared install pipeline modules, or make `install.ts` a thin compatibility re-export of the live implementation so there is only one install behavior to maintain.

issues_found.title: Client hook scripts are checked in as four identical source copies
issues_found.description: The package contains the template hook plus identical `.claude`, `.codex`, and `.cursor` hook copies; for example `fabric-hint.cjs` is present under `templates/hooks/` and all three client directories with identical hashes. The installer also copies templates into those client paths, so checked-in generated copies create a large duplication surface where hook changes can drift or require unnecessary multi-file edits.
issues_found.fix_direction: Keep `packages/cli/templates/hooks/**` as the only source of truth and generate/copy client hook directories during install or tests; if fixture copies are needed, move them under explicit test fixtures and add a drift check rather than treating them as maintained source files.
